titleStmt.title: Letter from Justin McCarthy to Mrs. O'Rahilly, 20 June, 1916.
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Justin McCarthy

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a letter of condolence to Nancy O'Rahilly (1875-1961). He acknowledges receipt of £5, for which 30 Gregorian Masses will be celebrated for the repose of The O'Rahilly's soul. The monks prayed, on hearing of his death, that God would grant them all eternal rest and console their sorrowing friends.Nancy O'Rahilly was a member of the Provisional Council of Cumann na mBan at the founding in 1914 and the wife of Michael Joseph O'Rahilly, self-described as 'The' O'Rahilly (1875-1916). The O'Rahilly fought in the Easter Rising and was shot in Parnell/Moore Street on 28 April 1916 and died some time later. Nancy was pregnant at the time of her husband's death – the child was born three months later. Justin McCarthy was Abbot of Mount St. Joseph's Abbey, Roscrea, Co. Tipperary.

msIdentifier.collection: Humphreys Papers P106/120/57, Humphreys Papers P106/120/57

correspAction.placeName: Mount St. Joseph's Abbey, Roscrea, Co. Tipperary, Ireland.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Mount St. Joseph Abbey, Roscrea 20.6.16 Dear Mrs. O'Rahilly I am very thank-
ful for letter and cheque
Â£5 to hand this morning
and will have thirty
Gregorian Masses said for
the repose of your husband's
soul. We had prayers
here for him on hearing of
his death as he was known
to some here and his father
well known to many. May
God grant them all eternal
rest and console their sorrow-
ing friends Yours Sincerely
Justin McCarthy
Abbot
